variables.name: ulm_card_battery_attribute
variables.notes: If your entity provides the battery percent in an attribute (= not as an own sensor), fill in the attribute's name here. E.g. if you have sensor.livingroom_thermometer and the attribute for your battery power is sensor.livingroom_thermometer.attributes.battery_percent, you fill in battery_percent here.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_battery_level_danger
variables.notes: Changes the color of the Icon, if the battery level falls below the provided value. Must be higher than ulm_card_battery_battery_level_waring

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_battery_level_waring
variables.notes: Changes the color of the Icon, if the battery level falls below the provided value.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_battery_state_entity_id
variables.notes: Entity that holds the battery state (charging/discharging). If provided, the Icon will display the current status.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_charger_type_entity_id
variables.notes: Entity that holds the charger type (ac/wireless/none). This Entity replaces the need for the ulm_card_battery_battery_state_entity_id entity. If provided, the Icon will display the current charger type. This is only useful if you charge your devices Wireless and with cable.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_charging_animation
variables.notes: Weather to show charging animation or not. If set to true, ulm_card_battery_battery_state_entity_id needs to be defined and ulm_card_battery_charger_type_entity_id will be ignored.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_color_battery_level_danger
variables.notes: Color of icon if battery level is within the 'danger' zone.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_color_battery_level_ok
variables.notes: Color of icon if battery level is not within the 'danger' or 'warning' zone.

variables.name: ulm_card_battery_color_battery_level_warning
variables.notes: Color of icon if battery level is within the 'warning' zone.

variables.notes: Customize name

variables.notes: Set to true if using a binary battery sensor. Will ignore battery_warning percentage settings

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_eraycetinay_lock_battery_sensor_binary_low_state
variables.notes: Set whether the binary sensor turns to on or off when in low state

variables.notes: At what battery percentage should the low battery warning appear.

variables.notes: At what battery percentage should the very low battery warning appear.

variables.name: action

variables.name: navigation_path
variables.default: The navigation path for the call
variables.notes: Path to navigate to (e.g., /lovelace/0/) when action defined as navigate

variables.name: service
variables.default: The service to call the action on
variables.notes: Service to call (e.g., media_player.media_play_pause) when action defined as call-service

variables.name: service_data
variables.default: The service_data to call the action on
variables.notes: Service data to include (e.g., entity_id: media_player.bedroom) when action defined as call-service

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_delayed_starttime
variables.notes: What time should the washing machine or dryer start

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_job_progress
variables.notes: What is the current progress in the program in %

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_job_state
variables.notes: What is the current step in the program, weightSensing, wash, rinse, spin or drying

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_job_states
variables.default: List of maximum 5 states (name and icon) to show as job states
variables.notes: Define the job states of the washing machine of dryer (or any other machine that you wanna use)

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_label_configuring
variables.default: Any text, for example, the end result of all the settings
variables.notes: What label to show when the washing machine or dryer is being configured

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_label_idle
variables.default: Any text, for example the number of runs
variables.notes: What label to show when the washing machine or dryer is idle

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_label_running
variables.default: Any text, for example the remaining time of the current program

variables.notes: What is the current state of washing machine or dryer: none, run or pause

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_pause_action

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_power
variables.notes: Is the washing machine or dryer turned on?

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_remote_control
variables.notes: Can we control the washing machine or dryer remotely

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_start_action
variables.default: A collection of setting to change a value or start an action
variables.notes: This contains all the parameters to start the washing machine or dryer (See the next table)

variables.name: ulm_custom_card_washer_stop_action
variables.default: A collection of setting to change a value or start an action
variables.notes: This contains all the parameters to stop the washing machine or dryer (See the next table)

variables.default: [[[ if (typeof(entity) !== 'undefined' && entity !== undefined){ let device_class = entity.attributes.device_class; let lang = hass["language"]; let action = entity.attributes.hvac_action; let domain = entity.entity_id.substr(0, entity.entity_id.indexOf(".")); let mode = ((hass.resources && hass.resources[lang]) || (hass.resources && hass.resources.en) || {})["state_attributes." + domain + ".hvac_action." + action]; if(variables.ulm_show_last_changed){ let dt = new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat(lang, { style: "long" }); let delta = (Date.parse(entity.last_changed) - Date.now()) / 1000 let days = 0 if (delta > 1000) days = parseInt(delta / (60 * 60 * 24)); let hours = parseInt(Math.round(delta / (60 * 60)) % 24); let hours_unrounded = parseInt((delta / (60 * 60)) % 24); let minutes = parseInt(Math.round(delta / 60) % 60); let minutes_unrounded = parseInt((delta / 60) % 60); let seconds = parseInt(Math.round(delta) % 60); if (days != 0) return dt.format(days,"days"); else if (hours_unrounded != 0) return dt.format(hours,"hours"); else if (minutes_unrounded != 0) return dt.format(minutes,"minutes"); return dt.format(seconds,"seconds"); } if(device_class == ('timestamp' || 'date')){ let dt = new Intl.DateTimeFormat(lang, { dateStyle: "long", timeStyle: "short" }); let formatted_date = dt.format(Date.parse(entity.state)) return formatted_date; } if(entity.entity_id.startsWith("climate.")){ return helpers.localize(entity, entity.attributes.current_temperature) + ' • ' + helpers.localize(entity) + ((entity.state !='off' && mode !== undefined) ? ' (' + mode + ')' : ''); } return helpers.localize(entity) } ]]]

variables.name: ulm_translation_state_reverse
variables.default: [[[ if (typeof(entity) !== 'undefined' && entity !== undefined){ let state = entity.state; let def = ["unknown", "unavailable"]; let lang = hass["language"]; if (state === "on"){ var translation = ((hass.resources && hass.resources[lang]) || (hass.resources && hass.resources.en) || {})["state.default.off"]; } else if (state === "off"){ var translation = ((hass.resources && hass.resources[lang]) || (hass.resources && hass.resources.en) || {})["state.default.on"]; } if (def.includes(state)) { var translation = ((hass.resources && hass.resources[lang]) || (hass.resources && hass.resources.en) || {})["state.default." + state ]; } return translation ? translation : state; } ]]]
